name: National Health Insurance Research Database (NHIRD)
coverage: >23 million (>99% of population, since 1995)
notes: Universal single-payer claims; 70+ billion records and 3.4 billion medical images as of 2024, now including structured laboratory and imaging data.

name: Chang Gung Research Database (CGRD)
coverage: ~1.3M outpatients + ~200K inpatients per year (~6–10% of national population)
notes: Standardized electronic medical records from seven Chang Gung Memorial Hospitals; available since 2000 with ~1-month data latency.

name: Taiwan Biobank (TWB)
coverage: ~200,000 community participants (target 1 million)
notes: Population-based biomedical biobank linking genomic, lifestyle, and clinical data.

name: National Database of Health Insurance Claims and Specific Health Checkups (NDB)
coverage: ≈120 million — effectively the entire Japanese population (since 2008)
notes: Operated by the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare; nationwide insurance claims and specific health checkup records used for medical-cost optimization research.

name: JMDC Claims Database
coverage: ~13.5 million members (≈9.4% of national population, working-age skew)
notes: Operated by JMDC Inc.; receipts, health-checkup results, and enrolment records pooled from ~179 health insurance societies. Excellent longitudinal traceability (up to 17 years).

name: DeSC Database
coverage: ~9.3 million members (≈8% of population) across employer / national / late-stage elderly insurers (since 2014)
notes: Operated by DeSC Healthcare, Inc.; first commercial Japanese DB to span Employee Health Insurance, National Health Insurance, and Late-Stage Elderly Healthcare in a single dataset.

name: Medical Data Vision (MDV) Database
coverage: ≈48 million patients across 519 acute-care DPC hospitals (≈29% of acute-hospital admissions in Japan)
notes: Operated by Medical Data Vision Co., Ltd.; inpatient and outpatient DPC claims plus laboratory results — strong for in-hospital exposure, severity, and outcomes research.

name: Health Insurance Review and Assessment Service (HIRA)
coverage: ~50 million (essentially the entire Korean population under universal NHI)
notes: Independent agency that reviews claims for reimbursement appropriateness; provides researchers a separate access pathway to Korean NHI claims since 2009 (open data initiative 2013).

name: National Health Insurance Service – National Health Information Database (NHIS-NHID)
coverage: ~50 million (compulsory NHI, nationwide)
notes: Population-level claims with periodic national health screening linkage; complementary to HIRA, with stronger linkage to enrolment and health-check data.

name: Clinical Data Analysis and Reporting System (CDARS)
coverage: >7 million (Hospital Authority territory-wide, since 1995)
notes: Public-sector electronic medical records covering the great majority of Hong Kong residents.

name: Clinical Practice Research Datalink (CPRD)
coverage: ~60 million patients (~20% currently active)
notes: MHRA-administered UK primary-care EHR with linkage to Hospital Episode Statistics, ONS mortality, and other national datasets.

name: The Health Improvement Network (THIN)
coverage: ~17 million patients (~6% of UK population, since 2003)
notes: UK primary-care EHR drawn from contributing general practices; complementary to CPRD, mapped to international coding systems. Listed as a NeuroGEN partner database.

name: UK Biobank (UKB)
coverage: ~500,000 community participants aged 40–69 at recruitment (2006–2010)
notes: Population-based biomedical biobank linking genomic, lifestyle, imaging, and electronic medical record data. NeuroGEN partner biomedical database.

name: Public Health Scotland (ISD)
coverage: ~5.4 million (entire Scottish population)
notes: National prescribing, hospital admissions, and death-records system covering all NHS Scotland — a NeuroGEN partner data source.

name: PHARMO Database Network
coverage: ~7 million patients (~40% of the Dutch population)
notes: Linked pharmacy, primary care, and hospital records across multiple regions of the Netherlands; NeuroGEN partner data source.

name: Finnish healthcare registers (FinReg)
coverage: ~5.5 million (entire Finnish population)
notes: Nationwide population registries linking prescriptions, hospital discharges, special-reimbursement, and cause-of-death data — used in NeuroGEN.

name: Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme — 10% sample (PBS)
coverage: ~2.5 million (10% random sample of national PBS beneficiaries)
notes: Federal subsidised prescription medicine claims for Australia's national PBS — a NeuroGEN partner data source.

name: Victorian Linked Health Data (VLHD)
coverage: ~6.5 million (entire Victorian population)
notes: State-level Victorian admissions, deaths, and dispensing data linked into a single research dataset.

name: Medicare (20% sample)
coverage: ~13 million beneficiaries (20% random sample of ~65M Medicare population aged 65+)
notes: US federal insurance for those aged 65+ and selected disability groups; widely used in NeuroGEN multi-database studies.

name: Medicaid
coverage: ~70 million beneficiaries (US public insurance for low-income / disability)
notes: Federal–state public insurance programme used as a partner data source in NeuroGEN multi-database collaborations.
